# on-chromeos.pages.dev

A fork of ChromeOS built entirely with modern web technologies.  
This project recreates the windowed desktop experience of ChromeOS in a browser, featuring draggable windows, material design components, and embedded web applications.

## Overview

This is a Web-based desktop environment that simulates the ChromeOS window manager.  
It uses native Web Components (LitElement/MWC) to implement a draggable, resizable window system that hosts embedded web apps (e.g., a calculator) inside iframes.

## Key Features

- **Desktop-like Window Manager** – Draggable, movable windows with title bars.
- **Material Web Components** – Uses MWC (Material Web Components) for UI elements like icon buttons and elevation effects.
- **Embedded Web Apps** – Runs external web applications (e.g., calculator) inside sandboxed iframes.
- **ChromeOS-inspired UI** – Mimics the look and feel of ChromeOS with a modern Material Design aesthetic.
- **Lightweight & Portable** – Runs entirely in the browser without native dependencies.

## Architecture

- **Base Framework**: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript with ES Modules.
- **UI Library**: Material Web Components (`mwc.js`) from Google's MWC library.
- **Component Model**: LitElement-based custom elements.
- **Window Logic**: Custom drag-and-drop implementation (`window.js`) that handles window positioning, minimize/maximize/close actions.
- **External Content**: Embedded applications are loaded via `<iframe>` (e.g., `https://gcalc.pages.dev/`).

## Technologies Used

- **Web Components** (Custom Elements v1, Shadow DOM)
- **Lit** (LitElement & lit-html for reactive UI)
- **Material Design 3** (via MWC)
- **CSS Custom Properties** (theming)
- **ES Modules** (native JavaScript module loading)

## Related Links

- [Live Demo](https://on-chromeos.pages.dev/)
- [Calculator App (embedded)](https://gcalc.pages.dev/)
- [Google Fonts (Roboto)](https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto:wght@400;500&display=swap)
- [Material Symbols](https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined)

## Purpose

This project demonstrates how a full-featured desktop environment can be implemented using only open web standards. It serves as a reference for building window managers, embedded application hosts, and ChromeOS-like interfaces without proprietary technologies.
